LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF BEHNAM & ASSOCIATES, INC'S MOTION IN OPPOSITION TO APPLICATIONS FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT OF DOUBLE-D ENGINEERING IS DENIED; PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE TO CARRY ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS NOT IN GOOD FAITH; THEREFORE, THE APPLICATION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH (BETWEEN CROSS-DEFENDANT DOUBLE-D ENGINEERING AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS WAH YEN REAL ESTATE COMPANY, NELLIE YOUNG AND DAVID HOM) IS GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (301/PJB) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Opposition To Applications For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Of Double-D Engineering
Off Calendar Mar-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF BEHNAM & ASSOCIATES, INC'S MOTION IN OPPOSITION TO APPLICATIONS FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT OF DOUBLE-D ENGINEERING is denied; plaintiff has presented no evidence to carry its burden of showing that the settlement is not in good faith; therefore, the application for determination of good faith (between cross-defendant Double-D Engineering and cross-complainants Wah Yen Real Estate Company, Nellie Young and David Hom) is granted. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189
